name: God of Riffs: Battle for the Metalverse

reviews.review_title: Fun and Addictive; A Spooky surprise
reviews.review_description: I never expected to enjoy the game, especially since I am a very big metal fan. Businesses with skeletons and bats are surprisingly fun, and the story mode adds a good touch. When reduced on time, the arcade mode is perfect. The soundtrack fits well, although I want more rock hits. Some do not register hits, and the background feels empty, but overall, it is a solid, inexpensive game that has large ability.

reviews.review_title: For those about to rock we salute you 🤘
reviews.review_description:
God of Riffs might be the first rhythm game I've played that actually has a campaign, which really gives it something that other rhythm games don't have.
The campaign has good humor throughout and   establishes that the game isn't taking itself too seriously, even in the settings menu when trying to turn down the volume of your Skull headed guide,  he complains about being turned down.
The two enemy types, skeletons and flying skulls, appear at a good distance and for a somewhat inexperienced rhythm game player such as myself,  makes the game a good gateway into the genre for new players, just downward swing with those guitars and smash those enemies.
There's no worrying about angled swings or dodging incoming objects, I played through the game from the comfort of my couch from beginning to end.
The campaign feels pretty easy going stuff until the very final level, which to me resembles more of a drum solo than keeping a rhythm, but the game does warn you that beating the Lord of Silence isn't about to be easy and it certainly wasn't joking, your shoulders are going to be burning by it's crescendo.
You also have arcade mode,all songs and difficulties ranging from easy to expert, I'm an okay rhythm game player but hard mode represents just the right spot for a challenge without making me feel like my shoulders are going to pop out of their sockets.
Now there are a few bugs, you get yellow skeletons and skulls which build up a power up that increases your score for a short while, the flying skulls seem to disappear before they reach the line as they should, which ends the streak you obviously need for a high score.
There is also a bug that causes some flying skulls to become invisible, I've set some high scores but I'm going to wait for those two issues to be fixed before I have a go at setting more.
Even with those two issues it doesn't take away the enjoyment of the game, I'd just like a few more tracks, maybe a few more guitar types and maps and I'd probably bump my rating up to 5 stars.
Fun to workout and love the music!  Between this and the dev's other game, Exercise Your Demons, both will gamify your workouts :)
Sometimes it would just not register a hit   which was really frustrating

I'm a big fan of rhythm typ games like beatsaber. I do play competive beatsaber so I play alot of beatsaber and games like beatsaber. God of riffs is definitely different from the rest of them. 

Game play 10/10

One thing I really like about this game is that your fighting enemy's skeletons/skelly bats. And the different maps and axes u can pick from are pretty nice. That's one thing that is really nice is that u can coastomize your look or change the map being able to change your axe is pretty nice cuz who doesn't like to play in style 😉. The game i will admit is a little more tricky than like boomboxvr and beatsaber. Witch i like but I'm not really into metal and rock but I like the idea.

Maps/customization 9.5/10

The different axes u can choose from and Maps are nice tho theirs not much since it's still new as of (Nov 8 2024) so there's not a hole lot to choose from but the devs will more than likely add more stuff in the future. Also gota say my favorite map is the lava one when u use the boost multiply the demon beings to aggressively bang his head 😆.

Songs 9/10

I can't hate on this one to much cuz it's  a rock/metal game lol I'm not into this type of music but the songs arnt bad. But do gota point out that there is already a good amount of songs since it's new. So for a game that's new to have this amount of songs is pretty nice.

Difficulty 9.5/10

Like I said before this one is definitely a different experience from beatsaber and other rhythm vr games witch is what makes this game fun and expet is definitely for those expet demon slayers 😈

reviews.review_title: The best way to break a sweat
reviews.review_description:
I have 0 problems with this game the voice acting was good the story was beautiful and he'll of a work out the music 100/10 and the vibe and layout of the maps absolutely beautiful 
I will keep continuing to play this and I hate beat Saber but this game was just wow keep up the great work also hear me out jack black as a voice actor for this game lol

This game is a lot of fun for fans of best saber who also are metal fans(me) I really enjoyed smashing skills and demons with my axes. There is also an awesome overdrive mode that adds a score multiplier and leaderboards for the competitive types. It's nice to see your name next to friends when completing the songs. I'm hoping for more licensed music. The story mode has a very cool comic book vibe that fits the metal theme. I would definitely recommend this game especially at this price point. Plenty of replayability.

Finally, something in VR with some balls! You will definitely work up a sweat and get your heart pumping and the arm swinging. It is so satisfying to bash these skulls with my axes, and the original artwork in here is spectacular in the new story mode. I would keep watching this Vyersoft team because they’re doing things right! This is exactly the kind of soundtrack I need for my workout. The kind that says you can’t stop swinging or else you’re gonna get all these bones in your mouth!

The vibes are good, great style, and the music is AWESOME. The songs cover quite a wide range of rock, and I commend them heavily for that. The art in the story is pretty great as well. But, the hit detection is definitely quite bad, especially for the flying enemies. There's also quite a lot of visual clutter with the enemies and their debris, so it's hard to make out what's what and devolved into flailing at many points. Charting isn't something I know all too well, but it also didn't feel great on most levels, nor the accuracy of the "perfect" timings. 

If you like the vibes of the game, it's a reasonable buy to experience it once. The music is stellar, and I can recommend it if either of those are your main draw, just expect to end up resorting to flailing some in order to experience it all. If you're an invested rhythm game player though, you'll be quite disappointed by the gameplay and clunkiness.

The game is, at best, mediocre. The graphics are very much "early quest 2" tier, that is - kinda blur, low polygon, low res textures one. You can see this especially on the weapons, that are super blocky and simple. The campaign mode is a nice idea, with cutscenes in between zones. The thing is, though, that these cutscenes are simply a 2D short still scene "animations" with narrator readings in a really low effort "cinema" scene. It all gives off a quite cheap vibe, even though there is some effort and will visible.


As for the gameplay, it takes upon the beatsaber lightsaber recipe and replaces these with one-sided axes. It certainly is different, but a) it is nothing particularly new, b) it kind of sucks because it lacks the sort of "flow" state that you can get in good beatsaber maps, where you need to move your hands in certain ways. Here, you just mash it up and down, which might be on the whole "metal" theme, but IMO sucks. There is no skill curve besides moving your wrist up and down and positioning with your arms. No dodging mechanism, no blocking, nothing. Just up and down. There is some "boost" mode, but it doesn't do anything substantial in the gameplay. 


The music is okay, I guess? I used to be a metalhead, but I am one no longer, so I won't grade it. Some songs felt a bit too slow, but again, not judging. My biggest gripe, however, that IMO entirely ruins any music whatsoever, is the song the axes do when killing enemies. It is a loud "ding" like sound, similar to striking a trash can with a lid, that drowns the music. Conveniently, this sound is muted in the trailers :). But still audible in the raw gameplay footage.


In the end, I feel like this whole experience would fare better as a custom beatsaber map pack. Nothing new, nothing original. Kudos for trying tho.

The game is original and offers fun mechanics, there are different types of songs and levels and the difficulty increases gradually. Unfortunately the game is short and doesn't offer an additional level to have a little more fun.But overall the game is good and you can easily have a good hour of fun and sweat.

I absolutely love the theme of the game.  Slaying enemies to some rock and metal music is a great combo.  Honestly though it did get frustrating missing some enemies you think you should have hit.  The song and map selection is great I love the addition of took studio to change up the scenes.  My largest wish would be more content.  The difficulty isn’t that high at first but can get a little panic swingy at top difficulty on harder songs.  I would say it’s a great game from an awesome idea. A new content drop and few tweaks here and there could definitely rise in ranking.

I really like the music, but it needs more. DLC or ability to do custom songs would me great. 
I like the idea and story. Voice acting is good. 
Gameplay was good and fun. I was having a blast and getting a workout. 
It can get boring after awhile because it doesn’t have as much content as other rhythm games. 
It would be great if they added to the variety of gameplay too. Like directional arrows or dodging projectiles. Just anything to keep it interesting

I love rhythm games! I often play games like Beat Saber or Synth Riders. But this doesn't feel special. In the rhythm of metal songs you need to slash enemies with your swords.

It has a story mode that is a bit similar to how the story is told in Metal: Hellsinger through short animated videos in between the levels, but the story is mid. It isn't that interesting that it keeps getting me back into the game.

Then there is another problem: Content. It doesn't have that many songs and the reason why I played Beat Saber so much is because of mods and the unlimited content they provide.

It also lacks variety. Not only do the songs sound similar, which isn't bad in particular but combined with the lack of different enemies it always feels pretty similar.

Not to mention how easy the game is. Because the slashes aren't restricted to a direction, like in Beat Saber, I was able to pass the highest difficulty after 10 minutes.

It also lacks graphics. They feel very basic and more like Quest 1 era with no updates since that. It's okay but definitely not more than that.

But what is good? The songs are good in my opinion. If you like Metal the songs are definitely something that you'll also like. It isn't very hard metal more like a bit softer but still I liked it.

So even though I enjoyed the songs I don't recommend this game to anyone. It lacks too much in terms of content, variety and graphics.

reviews.review_title: lots of bugs but could be fixed soon
reviews.review_description: the game works awesome its just a matter of the amount of bugs such as enemy's not spawning

This game is a pretty awesome rhythm game! This game basically simulates you in the seat of a drum set. It is pretty simple when you start, but once you go on higher difficulties you become a drummer! When I tested this game, there was definitely bugs but it was really fun and such a workout. The story mode is only a hour long, and after there isn't to many other songs. But, the replayability of this game is insane. This game is super promising and I would totally recommend it.

reviews.review_title: You have my rock and my axe!
reviews.review_description:
Awesome exercise alternative with good songs and a feeling of power destroying your foes, I think this is one for the moments when you're thirsting for a more relaxed (but not easy either!) rythmn experience. 
Hack and slash foes in both a story arcade mode where you can pick difficulties and your favorite songs.
I believe the lack of perfection lies in the content, without lots of the cool songs to pick the game can probably become stale quickly, with less of a gameplay loop to keep you in it. 
As for the difficulty, while it can be tough if you want it that way, it's nowhere near the difficulty the competitor rhythm games will bring you. You can slash your weapons in any direction without problem and this can be a great fun thing or a bummer depending on who you ask.

reviews.review_title: Good idea and great game for what it is
reviews.review_description: This game is pretty good. I love the theme of rock music and just being able to listen to rock and hit enemies is enjoyable. The environments were really cool and were the thing bringing me back to the game. But there are some things that could be improved. In the beginning it felt pretty slow and repetitive which made me not like it as much. I wish there was a difficulty setting which you could change to make it fit your play style. Overall this game is pretty good and with time and effort put into this game it could be one of greats. This game has a great idea and is good for what it is.

reviews.review_title: Love this take on Rhythm games
reviews.review_description:
Love this take on Rhythm games. 

Metal for the win 🎸 🤘🏻 

Looking forward to checking out there new game also Exercise Your Demons 😈🤞🏻

The game is nice and the tutorial clearly explains everything that you need to know, both the story and arcade are nice but the only thing the game could improve is matching the music with the enemies a bit better especially during the higher difficulties.
